steps.title: Substitute and recognize the parabola
steps.content:
Since $g(t) = f(t) + 3$, we have:
$$g(t) = 55t - 2t^2 + 3 = -2t^2 + 55t + 3$$
The leading coefficient is negative, so the parabola opens downward and has a maximum value.

steps.title: Complete the square to get vertex form
steps.content:
Factor $-2$ from the quadratic terms:
$$g(t) = -2(t^2 - \frac{55}{2}t) + 3$$
To complete the square, add and subtract $(\frac{55}{4})^2$ inside the parentheses:
$$g(t) = -2\left[(t - \frac{55}{4})^2 - (\frac{55}{4})^2\right] + 3$$
Distribute the $-2$:
$$g(t) = -2(t - \frac{55}{4})^2 + 2(\frac{55}{4})^2 + 3$$

steps.title: Extract the maximum value
steps.content:
The term $-2(t - \frac{55}{4})^2$ is always $\leq 0$, with its maximum value \$0$ when $t = \frac{55}{4}$. Therefore:
$$\max g(t) = 0 + 2(\frac{55}{4})^2 + 3 = 3 + 2(\frac{55}{4})^2$$
That matches choice B — B is correct.
